text:
<think>I should start by clarifying that most modern microwaves are designed to contain microwave radiation and are safe during normal operation.</think>
If you stand close to a modern, functioning microwave oven, it is generally safe because the appliance is designed to contain microwave radiation within the unit. 
<think>Next, I should explain why this is the case, mentioning the protective features of microwaves.</think>
Microwave ovens have metal shielding and a mesh on the door that prevents most microwave radiation from escaping while the device is in use. 
<think>I should mention that under normal circumstances, little to no radiation escapes.</think>
As long as the microwave is undamaged and used properly, only negligible amounts of microwave energy, if any, can leak out. 
<think>To be thorough, I should also note what could happen in the event of a malfunction or damage.</think>
If the microwave oven is damaged—for example, if the door seal is broken—there could be a risk of microwave leakage, which might potentially cause harm such as burns. 
<think>Finally, I should summarize and reassure the reader about everyday use.</think>
In everyday situations with a well-maintained microwave, standing close to it poses no significant health risk.
